title: alyssum alpestre subsp. serpyllifolium
intro: Alyssum alpestre subsp. serpyllifolium, also known as mountain sweet alyssum or alpine candytuft, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Alyssum alpestre subsp. serpyllifolium include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions when used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
